title: Receipt Matcher
summary: Extend a receipt attachment service to match receipts to likely transactions while sending ambiguous cases to manual review.

evidence.note: A realistic data-driven workflow that connects related records and requires MVP tradeoffs.

rubric.label: Codebase comprehension
rubric.excellent: Explains the current exact attachment behavior and data model.

rubric.label: Problem framing
rubric.excellent: Clarifies matching criteria, ambiguity, confidence, and review output.

rubric.label: AI direction
rubric.excellent: Uses AI for cases and review while owning scoring choices.

rubric.label: Command of solution
rubric.excellent: Defends why a receipt attaches, remains unmatched, or goes to review.

rubric.label: MVP judgment
rubric.excellent: Implements practical deterministic matching without overbuilding ML/fuzzy search.

rubric.label: Correctness
rubric.excellent: Handles amount/date/vendor matches, ambiguous candidates, and unmatched receipts.

rubric.label: Testing and validation
rubric.excellent: Tests obvious, ambiguous, and negative cases.

rubric.label: Tradeoff reasoning
rubric.excellent: Names confidence threshold and production data quality limitations.

rubric.label: Communication and cadence
rubric.excellent: Keeps interviewer oriented around deterministic matching rules.

checkpoints.title: Read the code
checkpoints.prompt: Summarize how receipts are currently attached.

checkpoints.title: Frame ambiguity
checkpoints.prompt: What matching and ambiguity rules would you clarify?

checkpoints.title: MVP plan
checkpoints.prompt: What deterministic MVP will you build and test?

checkpoints.title: Risk check
checkpoints.prompt: What match case is most likely wrong?

checkpoints.title: Final explanation
checkpoints.prompt: Explain matching confidence, manual review, validation, and AI usage.
